channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Holy Year is about to start in Rome. Here's what you need to know
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday formally inaugurates the 2025 Holy Year, reviving an ancient church tradition encouraging the faithful to make pilgrimages to Rome, amid new security fears following a Christmas market attack in Germany.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia's Putin holds talks with Slovakian PM Fico, in a rare visit to Moscow by an EU leader
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Sunday hosted Slovakia’s prime minister, Robert Fico, in a rare visit to the Kremlin by an EU leader since Moscow's all-out invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 10 are killed as a small plane crashes into a Brazilian town popular with tourists
item.description: A small plane crashed into a Brazilian town that's popular with tourists on Sunday, killing all 10 passengers on board and injuring more than a dozen people on the ground, Brazil's Civil Defense Agency said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least 22 as Vatican envoy visits Christians for pre-Christmas Mass
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip overnight and into Sunday killed at least 22 people, including five children, Palestinian medical officials said, while Gaza's small Christian community celebrated a pre-Christmas Mass.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rome's iconic Trevi Fountain reopens after renovation work in time for the Jubilee Holy Year
item.description: ROME (AP) — Rome’s iconic Trevi Fountain reopened Sunday after three months of renovations, just in time for the inauguration of the 2025 Jubilee Holy Year that is expected to draw millions of visitors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian drones hit a Russian fuel depot for the second time this month
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian drones struck a major Russian fuel depot for the second time in just over a week on Sunday, according to a senior Russian regional official, as part of a “massive” cross-border attack on fuel and energy facilities that Kyiv says supply Moscow's military.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: German Christmas market attack suspect in custody under murder charges
item.description: The man suspected of driving a car through crowds at a German Christmas market is in custody on murder charges, police said Sunday. On Friday, Taleb al-Abdulmohsen, a resident of Germany originally from Saudi Arabia, allegedly rammed a car into a busy holiday market in the German city of Magdeburg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: How to track Santa's flight around the world this year
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: Doctors Without Borders points to 'clear signs of ethnic cleansing' by Israel in Gaza
item.description: Doctors Without Borders said Israel is committing “clear signs of ethnic cleansing” in its war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power after key ally vows to topple him
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power next year after NDP leader Jagmeet Singh said he would move to bring down the minority Liberal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. universities have issued guidance to their international students urging them to be back on campus before the Jan. 20 inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Malaysia resuming search for MH370 wreckage
item.description: Malaysian officials approved a third probe into the Indian Ocean with the goal of recovering wreckage from flight MH370, which disappeared more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US officials travel to Syria in search of missing Americans, meet with rebel leaders
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in Syria making the first face-to-face contact with rebel groups that took over the country in a lightning offensive earlier this month, their mission aimed at searching for kidnapped American journalist Austin Tice and other missing Americans.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs if EU doesn't buy more oil, gas
item.description: President-elect Trump on Friday threatened tariffs in the European Union (EU) unless the bloc purchases large quantities of American oil and gas to make up for its growing deficit with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Italian artisans make around 100K bulbs for the signature light display at Singapore's Christmas Wonderland
item.description: Christmas Wonderland is a festive event that happens annually in Singapore. The festival is complete with breathtaking illuminations, activities, food and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran expands weaponization capabilities critical for employing nuclear bomb
item.description: A new report presented by the National Council of Resistance of Iran claims Tehran has expanded attempts to secretly develop weaponization capabilities for its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to travel to Italy for meetings with pope, Meloni in January
item.description: President Biden will travel to Italy in January for meetings with Pope Francis, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Italian President Sergio Mattarella. The trip, from Jan. 9 to 12, will likely be his last foreign trip as president before President-elect Trump is sworn in on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon says the number of US troops in Syria is much higher than previously reported
item.description: The Pentagon said Thursday that there are about 2,000 U.S. Troops in Syria, a number that is more than double the 900 troops reported previously by the DoD for months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli airstrikes target Yemen's Houthi-controlled capital of Sanaa, port city Hodeida
item.description: Israeli airstrikes targeting Yemen's Houthi-controlled capital, Sanaa, and Red Sea port city Hodeida left energy facilities ablaze in response to missiles launched earlier by the Houthis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation
